tasks.title: Create foundational runner plugin trait and execution framework
evidence: User request: Create foundational plugin system to enable isolating all runners into plugins for easier runner-specific customization.
crates/ralph/src/plugins/manifest.rs:33-44: Current RunnerPlugin manifest only has bin, supports_resume, default_model - lacks execution/response-parsing traits.
crates/ralph/src/plugins/registry.rs:54-75: Registry can resolve runner binary but delegates execution to hardcoded run_* functions.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/runners.rs:47-491: All runner execution is hardcoded (run_codex, run_claude, run_kimi, etc.) instead of plugin-driven.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/cli_spec.rs:27-138: CLI option mapping is hardcoded per runner (apply_codex_options, apply_claude_options, etc.).
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/response.rs:19-87: Response parsing is hardcoded per runner with runner-specific extract functions.
crates/ralph/src/contracts/runner.rs:21-32: Runner enum has Plugin(String) variant but no standardized trait for plugin execution.
plan: Design RunnerPlugin trait with methods for: command building, CLI option mapping, response parsing, and metadata (default model, supports resume).
Create PluginExecutor that delegates to RunnerPlugin implementations instead of hardcoded run_* functions.
Design response parsing trait (ResponseParser) with runner-specific implementations for extracting assistant text from JSON streams.
Create built-in plugin implementations for existing runners (codex, claude, kimi, etc.) that implement RunnerPlugin trait.
Refactor runner execution dispatch to use plugin registry: look up RunnerPlugin by runner ID, delegate command building and execution.
Update PluginManifest to support optional response format hints for external plugins.
Add tests for the plugin trait system and built-in implementations.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming all tests pass.
tasks.request: create tasks to have all runners isolated and integrated into the plugin system approach. If we need a task to make the plug-in system more foundational and integrated first that is fine, make sure this task is first if so.

tasks.title: Isolate codex runner into its own plugin for easier runner-specific customization
evidence: User request: Isolate all runners into plugins for easier runner-specific customization.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/runners.rs:47-117: Codex has dedicated run_codex() and run_codex_resume() functions with exec/resume subcommands.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/cli_spec.rs:27-47: Codex has unique CLI option mapping (apply_codex_global_options) with sandbox bypass and workspace-write modes.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/response.rs:142-154: Codex has unique response parsing (extract_codex_agent_message) for item.completed event type.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/runners.rs:62-69: Codex uses legacy_json_format and reasoning_effort options.
AGENTS.md:16-20: Code changes need tests covering success and failure modes.
plan: Wait for RQ-0657 to complete (foundational plugin trait framework).
Create CodexPlugin struct implementing the RunnerPlugin trait.
Implement command building: extract codex-specific logic from runners.rs:run_codex and run_codex_resume, preserving exec/resume subcommands and legacy_json_format.
Implement CLI option mapping: extract codex-specific options from cli_spec.rs:apply_codex_global_options (sandbox modes, bypass approvals).
Implement response parsing: extract codex-specific parsing from response.rs:extract_codex_agent_message for item.completed event type.
Register CodexPlugin as a built-in plugin with appropriate default model.
Refactor main runner execution to delegate to codex plugin when Runner::Codex is selected.
Add comprehensive tests for the codex plugin.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming all tests pass.
tasks.request: create tasks to have all runners isolated and integrated into the plugin system approach. We currently support codex, claude, pi, kimi, opencode, and cursor-agent.

tasks.title: Isolate kimi runner into its own plugin for easier runner-specific customization
evidence: User request: Isolate kimi runner code into its own 'plugin' to allow easier modification of runner-specific settings and tailor to each runner's unique properties/output responses.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/runners.rs:331-362: Kimi has dedicated run_kimi() function and build_kimi_command() helper (lines 493-516).
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/cli_spec.rs:111-121: Kimi has unique CLI option mapping (apply_kimi_options) using --yolo instead of standard --approval-mode.

crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/response.rs:11-15,87-138: Kimi has unique response parsing (KimiContent struct, extract_kimi_content, extract_kimi_assistant_text) for role=assistant format with content arrays.
crates/ralph/src/runner/model.rs:31: Kimi has unique default model 'kimi-for-coding'.
crates/ralph/src/plugins/: Existing plugin system supports runner plugins via PluginRegistry (registry.rs) and plugin manifest (manifest.rs).
AGENTS.md:72-78: Error handling uses thiserror for domain-specific matchable errors - relevant for runner plugin error types.
plan: Wait for RQ-0657 to complete (foundational plugin trait framework).
Create KimiPlugin struct implementing the RunnerPlugin trait from RQ-0657.
Implement command building: extract kimi-specific logic from runners.rs:build_kimi_command, preserving --session flag handling.
Implement CLI option mapping: extract kimi-specific options from cli_spec.rs:apply_kimi_options (kimi uses --yolo instead of --approval-mode yolo).
Implement response parsing: extract kimi-specific parsing from response.rs:extract_kimi_content for role=assistant format with content arrays.
Register KimiPlugin as a built-in plugin with default model 'kimi-for-coding'.
Refactor main runner execution to delegate to kimi plugin when Runner::Kimi is selected.
Add comprehensive tests for the kimi plugin: command building, response parsing, and integration.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming all tests pass, including new kimi plugin tests.
tasks.request: Isolate the code required for the kimi runner into it's own 'plugin'. Each runner has unique properties and output responses and isolating them will allow me to modify runner specific settings much easier going forward and tailor to each runner. You may keep reusing shared logic in situations where it truly DOES NOT need to be unique to the kimi runner.

tasks.title: Preserve timeout diagnostics when output-capture mutex is poisoned
evidence: crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs:198-205: wrap_output_handler_with_capture drops capture writes if mutex lock fails (including PoisonError).
crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs:299-311: timeout path reads capture via `capture.lock().map(...).unwrap_or_default()`, discarding PoisonError and losing captured output.
Operability impact: timeouts can lose diagnostic output, undermining safeguard dumps.
plan: Handle PoisonError explicitly with `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())` when locking capture for both writing and reading; log a warning when poison is detected.
Add a unit test that poisons the mutex (panic while holding lock), then invokes the handler and asserts capture still records output and does not panic.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Deduplicate TaskEditKey field formatting to prevent TUI/CLI drift
evidence: crates/ralph/src/tui/task_edit.rs:141-160: TaskEditKey -> value mapping for editing.
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs:630-658: TaskEditKey -> value mapping for preview formatting.
DRY risk: overlapping keys (Request/Duplicates/*At fields) are implemented twice and can drift.
plan: Introduce a shared helper for TaskEditKey field extraction/formatting (in queue module or on Task) and use it from both the TUI editor and the preview code.
Update both call sites to use the shared helper.
Add tests covering a representative Task to ensure both paths render the same values for shared keys.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Split oversized TUI app module (3347 LOC) into cohesive submodules
evidence: Command: wc -l crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs -> 3347 lines.
AGENTS.md guideline: files >1000 LOC should be split; >800 LOC requires explicit justification.
plan: Identify cohesive responsibilities inside app.rs (state, actions, event handling, IO, rendering adapters).
Extract into new internal modules under crates/ralph/src/tui/ (e.g., app_state.rs, app_actions.rs, app_events.rs), keeping public surface minimal.
Keep behavior stable by wiring through existing entrypoints and updating imports.
Add/adjust tests for extracted modules where behavior is tricky or under-tested.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Split oversized parallel run module (3241 LOC) into cohesive submodules
evidence: Command: wc -l crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs -> 3241 lines.
AGENTS.md guideline: files >1000 LOC should be split; >800 LOC requires explicit justification.
plan: Map responsibilities in parallel/mod.rs (scheduling, worker lifecycle, PR operations, merge policy, state persistence).
Extract cohesive internal modules (e.g., scheduler.rs, worker.rs, pr_ops.rs, state_io.rs) under crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/.
Keep behavior stable by leaving public APIs in mod.rs and delegating to submodules.
Add/adjust tests covering critical paths (state file reading/writing, PR record reconciliation, merge policy decisions).
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Warn on .env load errors (ignore NotFound only)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/main.rs:31: `dotenvy::dotenv().ok();` ignores parse/IO errors when a .env is present.
Repro: create an invalid .env in a temp dir and run `ralph --help` from that dir; current code emits no warning, making misconfiguration hard to diagnose.
plan: Change dotenv loading to ignore only NotFound; capture other errors and surface them as a warning (e.g., log after logger init, or print a single redacted warning to stderr).
Add an integration test that runs the ralph binary from a temp dir containing an invalid .env and asserts stderr contains a warning while exit code remains success.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
